question: A Camunda user task's `zeebe:formDefinition` sets `formId` to `configure-control-process` but does not set `bindingType`. The same form has been deployed multiple times with different revisions. Which version of the form does Tasklist render when the task activates?

options.text: The version that was deployed together with the currently running process, because `deployment` is the default `bindingType`.
The latest deployed version at the moment the user task is activated, because `bindingType` defaults to `latest` when not specified.
The earliest deployed version, because Tasklist locks the form on first deployment.
An incident is raised because `bindingType` is a required attribute on `zeebe:formDefinition`.

a.text: `deployment` is a valid value but it must be set explicitly; it is not the default for `bindingType`.

b.text: The docs state that if the `bindingType` attribute is not specified, `latest` is used as the default, so Tasklist resolves the form to its latest deployed version at activation time.

c.text: Tasklist does not lock a form to the earliest deployment; the default behaviour is to use the latest deployed version.

d.text: `bindingType` is optional. Omitting it is supported and falls back to the `latest` default.

explanation: For Camunda Forms linked via `formId`, the `bindingType` attribute selects which version is used. If it is not specified, the docs say `latest` is used as the default, so the version resolved at task activation is the latest deployed version of that form.
